title: Indian Pulitzer Winners
focus: Highlights Indian and Indian-American winners of the Pulitzer, with a deep dive into the first Indian winner, Gobind Behari Lal.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 103]
Notable winners include:
- Gobind Behari Lal (1937)
- Jhumpa Lahiri (2000)
- Geeta Anand (2003)
- Siddhartha Mukherjee (2011)
- Vijay Seshadri (2014), and
- photojournalists Danish Siddiqui, Adnan Abidi, Sanna Irshad Mattoo, Amit Dave, Dar Yasin, and Mukhtar Khan

[Page 104]
Gobind Behari Lal
In 1937, Gobind Behari Lal became the first Indian to win a Pulitzer. The Indian-American journalist won the award in the reporting category with four others, for coverage of science at the tercentenary of Harvard University. He interviewed the likes of Albert Einstein and Mahatma Gandhi.
A member of the Ghadar Party in America, Gobind Behari Lal was a postgraduate from University of California, Berkeley. Gobind Behari Lal was also awarded the Padma Bhushan in 1969.
